description_wiki: YMCA, sometimes regionally called the Y, is a worldwide youth organization based in Vernier, Canton of Geneva, Switzerland, with more than 64 million beneficiaries in 120 countries.

description_wiki: The Chouinard Art Institute was a professional art school founded in 1921 by Nelbert Murphy Chouinard (1879–1969) in the Westlake neighborhood of Los Angeles, California.

description_wiki: Central America is a subregion of North America.

description: continent located mainly in the southwestern quadrant of the Earth
description_wiki: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere.

description: terrestrial continent located in north-western Eurasia
description_wiki: Europe is a continent located entirely in the Northern Hemisphere and mostly in the Eastern Hemisphere.

description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description_wiki: The Netherlands, informally Holland, is a country in Northwestern Europe, with overseas territories in the Caribbean.

description: country in southwestern Europe with territories in Africa
description_wiki: Spain, officially the Kingdom of Spain, is a country in Southern and Western Europe with territories in North Africa.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: French art exhibition
description_wiki: The Salon d'Automne (French: [salɔ̃ dotɔn]; English: Autumn Salon), or Société du Salon d'automne, is an art exhibition held annually in Paris.

description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

graduate level university degree
description_wiki:
A Master of Fine Arts (MFA or M.F.A.)
is a terminal degree in fine arts, including visual arts, creative writing, graphic design, photography, filmmaking, dance, theatre, other performing arts and in some cases, theatre management or arts administration.

description: public library system of Detroit, Michigan, USA
description_wiki: The Detroit Public Library is the municipal public library system of the City of Detroit in Michigan, United States.

description: architectural structure in Washington, DC, USA
description_wiki: The Basilica of the National Shrine of the Immaculate Conception is a Catholic minor basilica and national shrine in Washington D.C. It is the largest Catholic church building in North America and the tallest habitable building in Washington, D.C. Its construction, in the Neo-Byzantine and Romanesque Revival styles, began in 1920.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: 1928 building housing the government of the city of Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: Los Angeles City Hall, completed in 1928, is the center of the government of the city of Los Angeles, California, and houses the mayor's office and the meeting chambers and offices of the Los Angeles City Council.

description: Catholic university located in Notre Dame, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: The University of Notre Dame du Lac (known simply as Notre Dame;   NOH-tər-DAYM; ND) is a private Catholic research university in Notre Dame, Indiana, United States.
